error.signature: I2C error: NACK received (AF flag set), address 0x68

verdict.summary: I2C slave does not acknowledge. Almost always wrong address (7-bit vs 8-bit confusion) or missing pull-ups.

dead_ends.action: Add more pull-up resistors in parallel
dead_ends.why_fails: Too-low resistance causes signal distortion. Standard: 4.7k for 100kHz, 2.2k for 400kHz.

dead_ends.action: Increase I2C clock speed to reduce NACK
dead_ends.why_fails: NACKs are not timing-related. Higher speed makes bus problems worse.
Some devices (EEPROMs) respond to scan with side effects like data corruption

workarounds.action: Verify 7-bit address - datasheets often list 8-bit (left-shifted) address
workarounds.how: Datasheet says 0xD0 -> 7-bit is 0x68. HAL uses 8-bit: HAL_I2C_Master_Transmit(hi2c, 0xD0, ...)

workarounds.action: Check hardware: SDA, SCL, pull-ups to VCC
workarounds.how: Both lines need pull-ups (4.7k to 3.3V). Idle state should be HIGH.

workarounds.action: Use logic analyzer to verify actual bus activity
